title: Heliconia buccinata
taxonomy: Heliconia buccinata is a species of flowering plant in the family Heliconiaceae. It belongs to the genus Heliconia and is native to Central and South America. Heliconia buccinata is a type of heliconia, commonly known as lobster claw, due to its brightly colored, claw-like flowers. The plant is also referred to as a member of the order Zingiberales. Heliconia buccinata is closely related to other species within the same family and genus, sharing similarities in its plant structure and growth habits.
